message: Update dependencies
patch:
commit 1a9c5e3db8d06c094a469b68da1432d9de3e8c1e
Author: Jonathan Calmels <jcalmels@nvidia.com>
Date:   Wed Feb 1 16:00:33 2023 -0800

    Update dependencies
---
 .gitmodules        |  2 +-
 deps/Makefile      | 17 ++++++++++++-----
 deps/libbsd        |  2 +-
 deps/linux-headers |  2 +-
 deps/makeself      |  2 +-
 deps/musl          |  2 +-
 6 files changed, 17 insertions(+), 10 deletions(-)

diff --git a/.gitmodules b/.gitmodules
index 249ea80..c7f86d1 100644
--- a/.gitmodules
+++ b/.gitmodules
@@ -3,7 +3,7 @@
 	url = https://github.com/megastep/makeself.git
 [submodule "deps/libbsd"]
 	path = deps/libbsd
-	url = https://anongit.freedesktop.org/git/libbsd.git
+	url = https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/libbsd/libbsd.git
 [submodule "deps/musl"]
 	path = deps/musl
 	url = git://git.musl-libc.org/musl
diff --git a/deps/Makefile b/deps/Makefile
index 3ff944b..414ff4b 100644
--- a/deps/Makefile
+++ b/deps/Makefile
@@ -1,11 +1,11 @@
 unexport prefix exec_prefix bindir libdir sysconfdir DESTDIR
 
-MAKESELF_VERSION     := 2.4.0
-LIBBSD_VERSION       := 0.9.1
-MUSL_VERSION         := 1.1.24
-LINUX_HEADER_VERSION := 4.4.2-4
+MAKESELF_VERSION     := 2.4.5
+LIBBSD_VERSION       := 0.11.7
+MUSL_VERSION         := 1.2.3
+LINUX_HEADER_VERSION := 4.19.88-1
 
-.PHONY: all libbsd makeself musl linux-headers clean
+.PHONY: all libbsd makeself musl linux-headers clean update
 .DEFAULT_GOAL := all
 
 ifdef CROSS_COMPILE
@@ -60,3 +60,10 @@ clean:
 	-cd musl && { git clean -f -d -x; $(MAKE) distclean; git checkout .; $(RM) .stamp; }
 	-cd linux-headers && { git clean -f -d -x; $(RM) .stamp; }
 	rm -rf dist
+
+update: clean
+	git submodule sync --recursive
+	cd makeself && { git fetch origin; git checkout -f release-$(MAKESELF_VERSION); }
+	cd libbsd && { git fetch origin; git checkout -f $(LIBBSD_VERSION); }
+	cd musl && { git fetch origin; git checkout -f v$(MUSL_VERSION); }
+	cd linux-headers && { git fetch origin; git checkout -f v$(LINUX_HEADER_VERSION); }

author: Jonathan Calmels
message: Add support for OCI format
patch:
commit a7493bf44dd3c5e83d6732f39bdc711b963a1a69
Author: Jonathan Calmels <jcalmels@nvidia.com>
Date:   Wed Feb 1 13:08:10 2023 -0800

    Add support for OCI format
---
 src/docker.sh | 9 +++++----
 1 file changed, 5 insertions(+), 4 deletions(-)

diff --git a/src/docker.sh b/src/docker.sh
index d968ccc..9d8bc73 100644
--- a/src/docker.sh
+++ b/src/docker.sh
@@ -130,8 +130,9 @@ docker::_download() {
         image="library/${image}"
     fi
 
-    local layers=() missing_digests=() cached_digests= manifest= config=
-    local req_params=("-H" "Accept: application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json")
+    local req_params=() layers=() missing_digests=() cached_digests= manifest= config=
+    local accept_manifest_list=("-H" "Accept: application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.list.v2+json, application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json")
+    local accept_manifest=("-H" "Accept: application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json, application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json")
     local url_manifest="${curl_proto}://${registry}/v2/${image}/manifests/${tag}"
     local -r url_digest="${curl_proto}://${registry}/v2/${image}/blobs/"
 
@@ -143,7 +144,7 @@ docker::_download() {
 
     # Attempt to use the image manifest list if it exists.
     common::log INFO "Fetching image manifest list"
-    CURL_IGNORE="401 404" common::curl "${curl_opts[@]}" "${req_params[@]/manifest/manifest.list}" -- "${url_manifest}" \
+    CURL_IGNORE="401 404" common::curl "${curl_opts[@]}" "${accept_manifest_list[@]}" "${req_params[@]}" -- "${url_manifest}" \
       | common::jq -r "(.manifests[] | select(.platform.architecture == \"${arch}\") | .digest)? // empty" \
       | common::read -r manifest
 
@@ -153,7 +154,7 @@ docker::_download() {
 
     # Fetch the image manifest.
     common::log INFO "Fetching image manifest"
-    common::curl "${curl_opts[@]}" "${req_params[@]}" -- "${url_manifest}" \
+    common::curl "${curl_opts[@]}" "${accept_manifest[@]}" "${req_params[@]}" -- "${url_manifest}" \
       | common::jq -r '(.config.digest | ltrimstr("sha256:"))? // empty, ([.layers[].digest | ltrimstr("sha256:")] | reverse | @tsv)?' \
       | { common::read -r config; IFS=$'\t' common::read -r -a layers; }

author: Leopold Talirz
message: docs: import from Azure Container Registry
patch:
commit 9c6e979059699e93cfc1cce0967b78e54ad0e263
Author: Leopold Talirz <leopold.talirz@gmail.com>
Date:   Wed Aug 31 17:45:39 2022 -0700

    docs: import from Azure Container Registry
    
    Add credentials configuration for importing from Azure Container Registry.
---
 doc/cmd/import.md | 5 +++++
 1 file changed, 5 insertions(+)

diff --git a/doc/cmd/import.md b/doc/cmd/import.md
index 0d86d7f..5d499d3 100644
--- a/doc/cmd/import.md
+++ b/doc/cmd/import.md
@@ -37,6 +37,11 @@ machine gcr.io login _json_key password $(jq -c '.' $GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTI
 
 # Amazon Elastic Container Registry
 machine 12345.dkr.ecr.eu-west-2.amazonaws.com login AWS password $(aws ecr get-login-password --region eu-west-2)
+
+# Azure Container Registry with ACR refresh token
+machine myregistry.azurecr.io login 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000 password $(az acr login --name myregistry --expose-token --query accessToken  | tr -d '"')
+# Azure Container Registry with ACR admin user
+machine myregistry.azurecr.io login myregistry password $(az acr credential show --name myregistry --subscription mysub --query passwords[0].value | tr -d '"')
 ```
 
 ### Supported schemes

author: Felix Abecassis
message: Update 50-sharp.sh for NVIDIA SHARP 2.7.0
patch:
commit 61b424f46bd2be616c42c5ef8e9883e238e8b69b
Author: Felix Abecassis <fabecassis@nvidia.com>
Date:   Wed Jun 29 10:11:51 2022 -0700

    Update 50-sharp.sh for NVIDIA SHARP 2.7.0
    
    Signed-off-by: Felix Abecassis <fabecassis@nvidia.com>
---
 conf/hooks/extra/50-sharp.sh | 12 ++++++++++--
 1 file changed, 10 insertions(+), 2 deletions(-)

diff --git a/conf/hooks/extra/50-sharp.sh b/conf/hooks/extra/50-sharp.sh
index 5b01601..516e118 100755
--- a/conf/hooks/extra/50-sharp.sh
+++ b/conf/hooks/extra/50-sharp.sh
@@ -31,11 +31,19 @@ fi
 
 sharp_sock=$(awk '($8 ~ "^@sharpd" && $8 != "@sharpd_unix_sock"){print $8}' /proc/net/unix)
 
-if [ -z "${sharp_sock}" ]; then
+# The sharpd daemon has been removed in NVIDIA SHARP 2.7.0:
+# https://docs.nvidia.com/networking/display/SHARPv270/Running+NVIDIA+SHARP+Aggregation+Manager+%28AM%29+Daemons
+# To trigger this hook when sharpd is not present, we check whether --network=sharp was used in the Slurm job.
+#
+# SLURM_NETWORK is not set consistently (https://bugs.schedmd.com/show_bug.cgi?id=14336), so a patch is
+# currently needed: https://gitlab.com/nvidia/dlsyseng/slurm/-/commit/8060b224960e5cd036f3eae881de42eccd40d399
+if [ -z "${sharp_sock}" ] && [ "${SLURM_NETWORK:-}" != "sharp" ]; then
     exit 0
 fi
 
-printf "SHARP_COLL_SHARPD_SOCKET_NAME=%s\n" "${sharp_sock#@}" >> "${ENROOT_ENVIRON}"
+if [ -n "${sharp_sock}" ]; then
+   printf "SHARP_COLL_SHARPD_SOCKET_NAME=%s\n" "${sharp_sock#@}" >> "${ENROOT_ENVIRON}"
+fi
 
 # Disable SHARP lazy locking and initialization.
 # https://docs.mellanox.com/display/sharpv214/Mellanox+SHARP+Collective+Library

-sudo yum install -y https://github.com/NVIDIA/enroot/releases/download/v3.4.0/enroot-hardened-3.4.0-1.el7.${arch}.rpm

 ```

diff --git a/pkg/rpm/SPECS/enroot.spec b/pkg/rpm/SPECS/enroot.spec

@@ -58,6 +58,9 @@ allows them to import and convert container images directly.

author: Tobias Pape
message: [rpm] Fix script ordering
patch:
commit 6b532300abe333ad3248ba325368a23219458370
Author: Tobias Pape <tobias@netshed.de>
Date:   Wed Nov 10 15:14:00 2021 +0100

    [rpm] Fix script ordering
    
    since %perun of old runs _after_ %post of new, upgrades break.
    Use %posttrans insead, which runs last
    
    Signed-off-by: Tobias Pape <tobias@netshed.de>
---
 pkg/rpm/SPECS/enroot.spec | 2 +-
 1 file changed, 1 insertion(+), 1 deletion(-)

diff --git a/pkg/rpm/SPECS/enroot.spec b/pkg/rpm/SPECS/enroot.spec
index 735c525..7b3d3fd 100644
--- a/pkg/rpm/SPECS/enroot.spec
+++ b/pkg/rpm/SPECS/enroot.spec
@@ -43,7 +43,7 @@ unprivileged sandboxes.
 
 This dependency package grants extra capabilities to unprivileged users which
 allows them to import and convert container images directly.
-%post -n %{name}+caps
+%posttrans -n %{name}+caps
 /usr/sbin/setcap cap_sys_admin+ep %{_bindir}/enroot-mksquashovlfs
 /usr/sbin/setcap cap_sys_admin,cap_mknod+pe %{_bindir}/enroot-aufs2ovlfs
 %preun -n %{name}+caps

author: Jonathan Calmels
message: Workaround squashfs-tools issue when RLIMIT_NOFILE is too high
patch:
commit 8a8d2796897c8dd42210ab9d66cf4b905b073947
Author: Jonathan Calmels <jcalmels@nvidia.com>
Date:   Tue Oct 5 17:17:40 2021 -0700

    Workaround squashfs-tools issue when RLIMIT_NOFILE is too high
    
    See: https://github.com/NVIDIA/enroot/issues/90
---
 src/runtime.sh | 2 ++
 1 file changed, 2 insertions(+)

diff --git a/src/runtime.sh b/src/runtime.sh
index 07b36fd..aa83dfa 100644
--- a/src/runtime.sh
+++ b/src/runtime.sh
@@ -416,6 +416,8 @@ runtime::create() {
 
     # Extract the container rootfs from the image.
     common::log INFO "Extracting squashfs filesystem..." NL
+    # XXX: https://github.com/NVIDIA/enroot/issues/90
+    [ $(ulimit -n) -gt $((2**26)) ] && ulimit -n $((2**26))
     unsquashfs ${TTY_OFF+-no-progress} -user-xattrs -d "${rootfs}" "${image}" >&2
     common::fixperms "${rootfs}"
 }

author: Tobias Pape
message: Allow for config drop-ins
patch:
commit 0d66136785d634562eab9774ecae61a82a11716c
Author: Tobias Pape <tobias@netshed.de>
Date:   Thu Sep 30 15:10:25 2021 +0200

    Allow for config drop-ins
    
    Enhancement to /etc/enroot/enroot.conf:
    If the directory /etc/enroot/enroot.conf.d exisits,
    also load all files named *.conf in the same manner as the .conf file
    in asciicographical order (modulo LC_COLLATE)
    
    Signed-off-by: Tobias Pape <tobias@netshed.de>
---
 Makefile                  |  7 +++++--
 conf/enroot.conf.d/README | 12 ++++++++++++
 doc/configuration.md      |  1 +
 enroot.in                 | 15 ++++++++++++++-
 4 files changed, 32 insertions(+), 3 deletions(-)

diff --git a/Makefile b/Makefile
index 5f193b4..0b09c55 100644
--- a/Makefile
+++ b/Makefile
@@ -38,7 +38,9 @@ UTILS := bin/enroot-aufs2ovlfs    \
          bin/enroot-switchroot    \
          bin/enroot-nsenter
 
-CONFIG := conf/enroot.conf
+CONFIGFILE := enroot.conf
+CONFIG := conf/$(CONFIGFILE)
+CONFIGINFO := conf/$(CONFIGFILE).d/README
 
 HOOKS := conf/hooks/10-aptfix.sh   \
          conf/hooks/10-cgroups.sh  \
@@ -125,7 +127,7 @@ depsclean:
 
 install: all
 	install -d -m 755 $(SYSCONFDIR) $(LIBDIR) $(BINDIR) $(DATADIR)
-	install -d -m 755 $(addprefix $(SYSCONFDIR)/, environ.d mounts.d hooks.d)
+	install -d -m 755 $(addprefix $(SYSCONFDIR)/, environ.d mounts.d hooks.d $(CONFIGFILE).d)
 	install -d -m 755 $(addprefix $(DATADIR)/, environ.d mounts.d hooks.d)
 	install -m 644 $(ENVIRON) $(SYSCONFDIR)/environ.d
 	install -m 644 $(MOUNTS) $(SYSCONFDIR)/mounts.d
@@ -134,6 +136,7 @@ install: all
 	install -m 644 $(MOUNTS_EXTRA) $(DATADIR)/mounts.d
 	install -m 644 $(CONFIG_EXTRA) $(DATADIR)
 	install -m 644 $(CONFIG) $(SYSCONFDIR)
+	install -m 644 $(CONFIGINFO) $(SYSCONFDIR)/$(CONFIGFILE).d
 	install -m 644 $(SRCS) $(LIBDIR)
 	install -m 755 $(BIN) $(UTILS) $(DEPS) $(BINDIR)
 
diff --git a/conf/enroot.conf.d/README b/conf/enroot.conf.d/README
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..c6ee020
--- /dev/null
+++ b/conf/enroot.conf.d/README
@@ -0,0 +1,12 @@
+# Drop-ins for enroot.conf
+#
+# All files in this directory named *.conf are read in shell expansion order
+# (ie, asciicographical, possibly influenced by LC_COLLATE)
+#
+# Any file to be processed must adhere to the same format as enroot.conf.
+# See there for details.
+#
+# If necessary, a numbering scheme (10-default.conf, 20-local.conf, 30-....conf)
+# might be beneficial.
+#
+# EOF
diff --git a/doc/configuration.md b/doc/configuration.md
index 71d851b..cdf666d 100644
--- a/doc/configuration.md
+++ b/doc/configuration.md
@@ -17,6 +17,7 @@ The following table describes standard paths used by the runtime:
 | `ENROOT_DATA_PATH` | `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/enroot` | Path to user container storage |
 | `ENROOT_TEMP_PATH` | `${TMPDIR}` | Path to temporary directory |
 
+When `enroot.conf` has been read, and if there is a directory `enroot.conf.d` next to `enroot.conf`, all files in that directory matching `*.conf` will be considered, too.
 
 ## Container configuration
 
diff --git a/enroot.in b/enroot.in
index 268ce38..c0de826 100644
--- a/enroot.in
+++ b/enroot.in
@@ -28,7 +28,7 @@ config::export() {
     [ -n "${3-}" ] && export "$1=$2" || export "$1=${!1:-$2}"
 }
 
-config::init() {
+config::load() {
     local -r file="$1" overwrite="${2-}"
 
     if [ -s "${file}" ] && ! "${BASH}" -n "${file}" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
@@ -47,6 +47,19 @@ config::init() {
    fi
 }
 
+config::init() {
+    local -r file="$1" overwrite="${2-}"
+
+    config::load "${file}" "${overwrite}"
+    if [ -d "${file}.d" ]; then
+      shopt -s nullglob
+      for dropin in "${file}.d"/*".conf"; do
+        config::load "${dropin}" "${overwrite}"
+      done
+      shopt -u nullglob
+    fi
+}
+
 config::fini() {
     for var in $(compgen -e "ENROOT_"); do
         if [[ "${!var}" =~ ^(no?|N[oO]?|[fF](alse)?|FALSE)$ ]]; then

author: Markus Opolka
message: Fix registry variable to support URLs with ports in the import
patch:
commit 067079ee4a8dec8420c91d445f0b7669d156886a
Author: Markus Opolka <markus@martialblog.de>
Date:   Thu Sep 30 09:50:52 2021 +0200

    Fix registry variable to support URLs with ports in the import
    
     - Without this change users cannot use the .credentials file when the Container registry uses a Port in the URL
     - Fixes #99
    
    Signed-off-by: Markus Opolka <markus@martialblog.de>
---
 src/docker.sh | 4 ++--
 1 file changed, 2 insertions(+), 2 deletions(-)

diff --git a/src/docker.sh b/src/docker.sh
index 52f4c3d..d968ccc 100644
--- a/src/docker.sh
+++ b/src/docker.sh
@@ -55,7 +55,7 @@ docker::_authenticate() {
     # If a user was specified, lookup his credentials.
     common::log INFO "Authenticating with user: ${user:-<anonymous>}"
     if [ -n "${user}" ]; then
-        if grep -qs "machine[[:space:]]\+${registry}[[:space:]]\+login[[:space:]]\+${user}" "${creds_file}"; then
+        if grep -qs "machine[[:space:]]\+${registry%:*}[[:space:]]\+login[[:space:]]\+${user}" "${creds_file}"; then
             common::log INFO "Using credentials from file: ${creds_file}"
             exec {fd}< <(common::evalnetrc "${creds_file}" 2> /dev/null)
             req_params+=("--netrc-file" "/proc/self/fd/${fd}")
@@ -279,7 +279,7 @@ docker::import() (
     # This is especially useful if the registry has been mistakenly specified as part of the image (i.e. nvcr.io/nvidia/cuda).
     if [ -s "${creds_file}" ]; then
         if [ -n "${registry}" ] && [ -z "${user}" ]; then
-            user="$(awk "/^[[:space:]]*machine[[:space:]]+${registry}[[:space:]]+login[[:space:]]+.+/ { print \$4; exit }" "${creds_file}")"
+            user="$(awk "/^[[:space:]]*machine[[:space:]]+${registry%:*}[[:space:]]+login[[:space:]]+.+/ { print \$4; exit }" "${creds_file}")"
         elif [ -z "${registry}" ] && [ -z "${user}" ] && [[ "${image}" == */* ]]; then
             user="$(awk "/^[[:space:]]*machine[[:space:]]+${image%%/*}[[:space:]]+login[[:space:]]+.+/ { print \$4; exit }" "${creds_file}")"
             if [ -n "${user}" ]; then

author: alan.souza
message: Import local images from podman
patch:
commit 37a519294df6ed4d423e1afd73ac933ed471989f
Author: alan.souza <aavbsouza@gmail.com>
Date:   Mon Sep 27 19:36:25 2021 -0300

    Import local images from podman
    
    Add option to import local images (dockerd - docker daemon) from podman
    local repositories
    
    Signed-off-by: alan.souza <aavbsouza@gmail.com>
    Closes: #97
---
 doc/cmd/import.md |  1 +
 enroot.in         |  3 ++-
 src/docker.sh     | 19 +++++++++++++------
 src/runtime.sh    |  2 +-
 4 files changed, 17 insertions(+), 8 deletions(-)

diff --git a/doc/cmd/import.md b/doc/cmd/import.md
index a9da9e1..e655bd0 100644
--- a/doc/cmd/import.md
+++ b/doc/cmd/import.md
@@ -8,6 +8,7 @@ Import a container image from a specific location.
  Schemes:
    docker://[USER@][REGISTRY#]IMAGE[:TAG]  Import a Docker image from a registry
    dockerd://IMAGE[:TAG]                   Import a Docker image from the Docker daemon
+   podman://IMAGE[:TAG]                    Import a Docker image from a local podman repository
 
  Options:
    -a, --arch    Architecture of the image (defaults to host architecture)
diff --git a/enroot.in b/enroot.in
index c7eb178..268ce38 100644
--- a/enroot.in
+++ b/enroot.in
@@ -165,7 +165,8 @@ enroot::usage() {
 		 Schemes:
 		   docker://[USER@][REGISTRY#]IMAGE[:TAG]  Import a Docker image from a registry
 		   dockerd://IMAGE[:TAG]                   Import a Docker image from the Docker daemon
-		
+		   podman://IMAGE[:TAG]                    Import a Docker image from a local podman repository
+
 		 Options:
 		   -a, --arch    Architecture of the image (defaults to host architecture)
 		   -o, --output  Name of the output image file (defaults to "URI.sqsh")
diff --git a/src/docker.sh b/src/docker.sh
index 05734fd..52f4c3d 100644
--- a/src/docker.sh
+++ b/src/docker.sh
@@ -338,14 +338,21 @@ docker::import() (
 docker::daemon::import() (
     local -r uri="$1"
     local filename="$2" arch="$3"
-    local image= tmpdir=
+    local image= tmpdir= engine=
 
-    common::checkcmd jq docker mksquashfs tar
+    case "${uri}" in
+    dockerd://*)
+        engine="docker" ;;
+    podman://*)
+        engine="podman" ;;
+    esac
+
+    common::checkcmd jq "${engine}" mksquashfs tar
 
     # Parse the image reference of the form 'dockerd://<image>[:<tag>]'.
     local -r reg_image="[[:alnum:]/._:-]+"
 
-    if [[ "${uri}" =~ ^dockerd://(${reg_image})$ ]]; then
+    if [[ "${uri}" =~ ^[[:alpha:]]+://(${reg_image})$ ]]; then
         image="${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"
     else
         common::err "Invalid image reference: ${uri}"
@@ -373,15 +380,15 @@ docker::daemon::import() (
     # Download the image (if necessary) and create a container for extraction.
     common::log INFO "Fetching image" NL
     # TODO Use --platform once it comes out of experimental.
-    docker create --name "${PWD##*/}" "${image}" >&2
+    "${engine}" create --name "${PWD##*/}" "${image}" >&2
     common::log
 
     # Extract and configure the rootfs.
     common::log INFO "Extracting image content..."
     mkdir rootfs
-    docker export "${PWD##*/}" | tar -C rootfs --warning=no-timestamp --anchored --exclude='dev/*' --exclude='.dockerenv' -px
+    "${engine}" export "${PWD##*/}" | tar -C rootfs --warning=no-timestamp --anchored --exclude='dev/*' --exclude='.dockerenv' -px
     common::fixperms rootfs
-    docker inspect "${image}" | common::jq '.[] | with_entries(.key|=ascii_downcase)' > config
+    "${engine}" inspect "${image}" | common::jq '.[] | with_entries(.key|=ascii_downcase)' > config
     docker::configure rootfs config "${arch}"
 
     # Create the final squashfs filesystem.
diff --git a/src/runtime.sh b/src/runtime.sh
index c551789..07b36fd 100644
--- a/src/runtime.sh
+++ b/src/runtime.sh
@@ -433,7 +433,7 @@ runtime::import() {
     case "${uri}" in
     docker://*)
         docker::import "${uri}" "${filename}" "${arch}" ;;
-    dockerd://*)
+    dockerd://* | podman://*)
         docker::daemon::import "${uri}" "${filename}" "${arch}" ;;
     *)
         common::err "Invalid argument: ${uri}" ;;

author: Jonathan Calmels
message: Ignore chmod error when attempting to fix permissions
patch:
commit 2ecbcd790799f3b664a9eb46ca330d71bdbecc71
Author: Jonathan Calmels <jcalmels@nvidia.com>
Date:   Tue Sep 28 16:42:41 2021 -0700

    Ignore chmod error when attempting to fix permissions
    
    Fixes: #98
---
 src/common.sh | 6 +++---
 1 file changed, 3 insertions(+), 3 deletions(-)

diff --git a/src/common.sh b/src/common.sh
index 92d9a69..c2d931e 100644
--- a/src/common.sh
+++ b/src/common.sh
@@ -69,7 +69,7 @@ common::rmall() {
     local -r path="$1"
 
     rm --one-file-system --preserve-root -rf "${path}" 2> /dev/null || \
-    { chmod -f -R +wX "${path}"; rm --one-file-system --preserve-root -rf "${path}"; }
+    { chmod -f -R +wX "${path}" || :; rm --one-file-system --preserve-root -rf "${path}"; }
 }
 
 common::mktmpdir() {
@@ -232,8 +232,8 @@ common::fixperms() {
 
     # Some distributions require CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE on several files and directories, fix these.
     # See https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=517575 for some context.
-    chmod -f -R u+rX "${path}"
-    find "${path}" -maxdepth 5 -type d ! -perm -u=w -exec chmod -f u+w {} \+
+    chmod -f -R u+rX "${path}" || :
+    find "${path}" -maxdepth 5 -type d ! -perm -u=w -exec chmod -f u+w {} \+ || :
 }
 
 common::getpwent() {

author: Tobias Pape
message: [rpm] Use absolut setcap path and rpm-known binary path
patch:
commit ff1be122907c801817d21c5f30bc90789038afc7
Author: Tobias Pape <tobias@netshed.de>
Date:   Thu Sep 23 13:01:19 2021 +0200

    [rpm] Use absolut setcap path and rpm-known binary path
    
    fixes #79
    
    Signed-off-by: Tobias Pape <tobias@netshed.de>
---
 pkg/rpm/SPECS/enroot.spec | 8 ++++----
 1 file changed, 4 insertions(+), 4 deletions(-)

diff --git a/pkg/rpm/SPECS/enroot.spec b/pkg/rpm/SPECS/enroot.spec
index c9d5693..1bfdf9b 100644
--- a/pkg/rpm/SPECS/enroot.spec
+++ b/pkg/rpm/SPECS/enroot.spec
@@ -44,11 +44,11 @@ unprivileged sandboxes.
 This dependency package grants extra capabilities to unprivileged users which
 allows them to import and convert container images directly.
 %post -n %{name}+caps
-setcap cap_sys_admin+pe "$(command -v enroot-mksquashovlfs)"
-setcap cap_sys_admin,cap_mknod+pe "$(command -v enroot-aufs2ovlfs)"
+/usr/sbin/setcap cap_sys_admin+ep %{_bindir}/enroot-mksquashovlfs
+/usr/sbin/setcap cap_sys_admin,cap_mknod+pe %{_bindir}/enroot-aufs2ovlfs
 %preun -n %{name}+caps
-setcap cap_sys_admin-pe "$(command -v enroot-mksquashovlfs)"
-setcap cap_sys_admin,cap_mknod-pe "$(command -v enroot-aufs2ovlfs)"
+/usr/sbin/setcap cap_sys_admin-ep %{_bindir}/enroot-mksquashovlfs
+/usr/sbin/setcap cap_sys_admin,cap_mknod-pe %{_bindir}/enroot-aufs2ovlfs
 %files -n %{name}+caps
 
 %build

author: Felix Abecassis
message: Fix pytorch hook for multi-node jobs
patch:
commit 46725c62267110745053b808f12a6f878ce208d3
Author: Felix Abecassis <fabecassis@nvidia.com>
Date:   Wed Sep 22 14:51:40 2021 -0700

    Fix pytorch hook for multi-node jobs
    
    With multi-node jobs, SLURM_STEP_TASKS_PER_NODE was not an integer
    value, this caused OMP_NUM_THREADS to not be set when it should have.
    
    Fixes #93
    
    Signed-off-by: Felix Abecassis <fabecassis@nvidia.com>
---
 conf/hooks/extra/50-slurm-pytorch.sh | 2 +-
 1 file changed, 1 insertion(+), 1 deletion(-)

diff --git a/conf/hooks/extra/50-slurm-pytorch.sh b/conf/hooks/extra/50-slurm-pytorch.sh
index 3247120..9ca1bd7 100755
--- a/conf/hooks/extra/50-slurm-pytorch.sh
+++ b/conf/hooks/extra/50-slurm-pytorch.sh
@@ -37,6 +37,6 @@ if [ -n "${SLURM_LOCALID-}" ] && ! grep -q "^LOCAL_RANK=" "${ENROOT_ENVIRON}"; t
 fi
 
 # Matching the behavior of torch.distributed.run: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/blob/v1.9.0/torch/distributed/run.py#L521-L532
-if [ "${SLURM_STEP_TASKS_PER_NODE:-1}" -gt 1 ] && ! grep -q "^OMP_NUM_THREADS=" "${ENROOT_ENVIRON}"; then
+if [ "${SLURM_NTASKS_PER_NODE:-1}" -gt 1 ] && ! grep -q "^OMP_NUM_THREADS=" "${ENROOT_ENVIRON}"; then
     printf "OMP_NUM_THREADS=1\n" >> "${ENROOT_ENVIRON}"
 fi

author: Felix Abecassis
message: Document OMP_NUM_THREADS modification from 718fa02
patch:
commit 84bdac1faae22a6701f0d843539912794ce7c427
Author: Felix Abecassis <fabecassis@nvidia.com>
Date:   Mon Aug 9 11:05:27 2021 -0700

    Document OMP_NUM_THREADS modification from 718fa02
    
    Signed-off-by: Felix Abecassis <fabecassis@nvidia.com>
---
 conf/hooks/extra/50-slurm-pytorch.sh | 1 +
 1 file changed, 1 insertion(+)

diff --git a/conf/hooks/extra/50-slurm-pytorch.sh b/conf/hooks/extra/50-slurm-pytorch.sh
index 6fe0e89..3247120 100755
--- a/conf/hooks/extra/50-slurm-pytorch.sh
+++ b/conf/hooks/extra/50-slurm-pytorch.sh
@@ -36,6 +36,7 @@ if [ -n "${SLURM_LOCALID-}" ] && ! grep -q "^LOCAL_RANK=" "${ENROOT_ENVIRON}"; t
     printf "LOCAL_RANK=%s\n" "${SLURM_LOCALID}" >> "${ENROOT_ENVIRON}"
 fi
 
+# Matching the behavior of torch.distributed.run: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/blob/v1.9.0/torch/distributed/run.py#L521-L532
 if [ "${SLURM_STEP_TASKS_PER_NODE:-1}" -gt 1 ] && ! grep -q "^OMP_NUM_THREADS=" "${ENROOT_ENVIRON}"; then
     printf "OMP_NUM_THREADS=1\n" >> "${ENROOT_ENVIRON}"
 fi

author: Jonathan Calmels
message: Use rbind to mount cgroups in case of nested trees
patch:
commit 9316c1bf31358f33e2e534aa67a98e5fd952f170
Author: Jonathan Calmels <jcalmels@nvidia.com>
Date:   Fri Aug 6 16:49:25 2021 -0700

    Use rbind to mount cgroups in case of nested trees
---
 conf/hooks/10-cgroups.sh | 4 ++--
 1 file changed, 2 insertions(+), 2 deletions(-)

diff --git a/conf/hooks/10-cgroups.sh b/conf/hooks/10-cgroups.sh
index e68de38..139b4b8 100755
--- a/conf/hooks/10-cgroups.sh
+++ b/conf/hooks/10-cgroups.sh
@@ -25,8 +25,8 @@ while IFS=':' read -r x ctrl path; do
     fi < /proc/self/mountinfo | { IFS=' ' read -r x x x root mount x || :; }
 
     if [ -n "${root}" ] && [ -n "${mount}" ]; then
-        printf "%s %s none x-create=dir,bind,nosuid,noexec,nodev,ro\n" "${mount}/${path#${root}}" "${mount}" >> "${ENROOT_MOUNTS}"
+        printf "%s %s none x-create=dir,rbind,nosuid,noexec,nodev,ro\n" "${mount}/${path#${root}}" "${mount}" >> "${ENROOT_MOUNTS}"
     fi
 done < /proc/self/cgroup
 
-printf "none /sys/fs/cgroup none bind,remount,nosuid,noexec,nodev,ro,rslave,nofail,silent\n" >> "${ENROOT_MOUNTS}"
+printf "none /sys/fs/cgroup none rbind,remount,nosuid,noexec,nodev,ro,rslave,nofail,silent\n" >> "${ENROOT_MOUNTS}"

author: Jonathan Calmels
message: Set OMP_NUM_THREADS=1 in the pytorch hook similar to torch distributed
patch:
commit 718fa023c2008f72cbeb4726bf18b93f1cb0d823
Author: Jonathan Calmels <jcalmels@nvidia.com>
Date:   Fri Aug 6 16:43:36 2021 -0700

    Set OMP_NUM_THREADS=1 in the pytorch hook similar to torch distributed
---
 conf/hooks/extra/50-slurm-pytorch.sh | 4 ++++
 1 file changed, 4 insertions(+)

diff --git a/conf/hooks/extra/50-slurm-pytorch.sh b/conf/hooks/extra/50-slurm-pytorch.sh
index e7dc3a3..6fe0e89 100755
--- a/conf/hooks/extra/50-slurm-pytorch.sh
+++ b/conf/hooks/extra/50-slurm-pytorch.sh
@@ -35,3 +35,7 @@ fi
 if [ -n "${SLURM_LOCALID-}" ] && ! grep -q "^LOCAL_RANK=" "${ENROOT_ENVIRON}"; then
     printf "LOCAL_RANK=%s\n" "${SLURM_LOCALID}" >> "${ENROOT_ENVIRON}"

+fi

Author: Jonathan Calmels <jcalmels@nvidia.com>

author: Jonathan Calmels
message: Add new extra hook for SHARP
patch:
commit 524b204abd886c2f85425cf9d53ad39dade4fb17
Author: Jonathan Calmels <jcalmels@nvidia.com>
Date:   Mon Apr 5 12:38:06 2021 -0700

    Add new extra hook for SHARP
---
 Makefile                     |  6 +++--
 conf/hooks/extra/50-sharp.sh | 58 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 2 files changed, 62 insertions(+), 2 deletions(-)

diff --git a/Makefile b/Makefile
index 71b475d..b09ea0a 100644
--- a/Makefile
+++ b/Makefile
@@ -50,8 +50,10 @@ HOOKS := conf/hooks/10-aptfix.sh   \
 
 CONFIG_EXTRA := conf/bash_completion
 
-HOOKS_EXTRA := conf/hooks/extra/50-slurm-pmi.sh \
-               conf/hooks/extra/50-slurm-pytorch.sh
+HOOKS_EXTRA := conf/hooks/extra/50-slurm-pmi.sh     \
+               conf/hooks/extra/50-slurm-pytorch.sh \
+               conf/hooks/extra/50-mig-config.sh    \
+               conf/hooks/extra/50-sharp.sh
 
 MOUNTS := conf/mounts/10-system.fstab \
           conf/mounts/20-config.fstab
diff --git a/conf/hooks/extra/50-sharp.sh b/conf/hooks/extra/50-sharp.sh
new file mode 100755
index 0000000..7851d26
--- /dev/null
+++ b/conf/hooks/extra/50-sharp.sh
@@ -0,0 +1,58 @@
+#! /usr/bin/env bash
+
+# Copyright (c) 2018-2021, NVIDIA CORPORATION. All rights reserved.
+#
+# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
+# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
+# You may obtain a copy of the License at
+#
+#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
+#
+# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
+# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
+# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
+# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
+# limitations under the License.
+
+set -eu
+
+source "${ENROOT_LIBRARY_PATH}/common.sh"
+
+common::checkcmd awk
+
+tac "${ENROOT_ENVIRON}" | grep "^MELLANOX_" | while IFS='=' read -r key value; do
+    [ -v "${key}" ] || export "${key}=${value}"
+done || :
+
+if [ "${MELLANOX_VISIBLE_DEVICES:-void}" = "void" ] || [ "${MELLANOX_VISIBLE_DEVICES}" = "none" ]; then
+    exit 0
+fi
+
+sharp_sock=$(awk '($8 ~ "^@sharpd" && $8 != "@sharpd_unix_sock"){print $8}' /proc/net/unix)
+
+if [ -z "${sharp_sock}" ]; then
+    exit 0
+fi
+
+printf "SHARP_COLL_SHARPD_SOCKET_NAME=%s\n" "${sharp_sock#@}" >> "${ENROOT_ENVIRON}"
+
+# Disable SHARP lazy locking and initialization.
+# https://docs.mellanox.com/display/sharpv214/Mellanox+SHARP+Collective+Library
+if ! grep -q "^SHARP_COLL_LOCK_ON_COMM_INIT=" "${ENROOT_ENVIRON}"; then
+    printf "SHARP_COLL_LOCK_ON_COMM_INIT=1\n" >> "${ENROOT_ENVIRON}"
+fi
+if ! grep -q "^SHARP_COLL_NUM_COLL_GROUP_RESOURCE_ALLOC_THRESHOLD=" "${ENROOT_ENVIRON}"; then
+    printf "SHARP_COLL_NUM_COLL_GROUP_RESOURCE_ALLOC_THRESHOLD=0\n" >> "${ENROOT_ENVIRON}"
+fi
+
+# https://docs.nvidia.com/deeplearning/nccl/user-guide/docs/env.html#nccl-collnet-enable
+if ! grep -q "^NCCL_COLLNET_ENABLE=" "${ENROOT_ENVIRON}"; then
+    printf "NCCL_COLLNET_ENABLE=1\n" >> "${ENROOT_ENVIRON}"
+fi
+
+if [ -n "${SLURM_STEP_NUM_NODES-}" ] && ! grep -q "^NCCL_SHARP_GROUP_SIZE_THRESH=" "${ENROOT_ENVIRON}"; then
+    printf "NCCL_SHARP_GROUP_SIZE_THRESH=%s\n" "${SLURM_STEP_NUM_NODES}" >> "${ENROOT_ENVIRON}"
+fi
+if [ -n "${OMPI_MCA_orte_num_nodes-}" ] && ! grep -q "^NCCL_SHARP_GROUP_SIZE_THRESH=" "${ENROOT_ENVIRON}"; then
+    printf "NCCL_SHARP_GROUP_SIZE_THRESH=%s\n" "${OMPI_MCA_orte_num_nodes}" >> "${ENROOT_ENVIRON}"
+fi

message: common::fixperms: handle unreadable dirs
patch:
commit dde82ef9c88b86594d60164dd0eb3957b1b9a2ea
Author: Luke Yeager <lyeager@nvidia.com>
Date:   Thu Feb 11 10:43:40 2021 -0800

    common::fixperms: handle unreadable dirs
    
    Fixes: #56
    Fixes: #57
---

 1 file changed, 2 insertions(+), 1 deletion(-)

diff --git a/src/common.sh b/src/common.sh
index 57a1d70..92d9a69 100644
--- a/src/common.sh
+++ b/src/common.sh
@@ -232,7 +232,8 @@ common::fixperms() {
 
     # Some distributions require CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE on several files and directories, fix these.
     # See https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=517575 for some context.
-    find "${path}" -maxdepth 5 \( -type d ! -perm -u+w -exec chmod -f u+w {} \+ \) -o \( ! -perm -u+r -exec chmod -f u+r {} \+ \)
+    chmod -f -R u+rX "${path}"
+    find "${path}" -maxdepth 5 -type d ! -perm -u=w -exec chmod -f u+w {} \+
 }
 
 common::getpwent() {

author: Jonathan Calmels
message: Add support for Docker registries with basic authentication (e.g. AWS ECR)
patch:
commit c54edbe64496949fb376a1cb76fb793598eb9069
Author: Jonathan Calmels <jcalmels@nvidia.com>
Date:   Mon Apr 5 04:40:42 2021 -0700

    Add support for Docker registries with basic authentication (e.g. AWS ECR)
    
    Fixes: #59
---
 doc/cmd/import.md |  6 +++++-
 src/common.sh     |  2 +-
 src/docker.sh     | 38 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++------------
 3 files changed, 32 insertions(+), 14 deletions(-)

diff --git a/doc/cmd/import.md b/doc/cmd/import.md
index b23097b..a9da9e1 100644
--- a/doc/cmd/import.md
+++ b/doc/cmd/import.md
@@ -29,9 +29,13 @@ machine authn.nvidia.com login $oauthtoken password <token>
 # DockerHub
 machine auth.docker.io login <login> password <passord>
 
-# Google Container Registry
+# Google Container Registry with OAuth
 machine gcr.io login oauth2accesstoken password $(gcloud auth print-access-token)
+# Google Container Registry with JSON
 machine gcr.io login _json_key password $(jq -c '.' $GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS | sed 's/ /\\u0020/g')
+
+# Amazon Elastic Container Registry
+machine 12345.dkr.ecr.eu-west-2.amazonaws.com login AWS password $(aws ecr get-login-password --region eu-west-2)
 ```
 
 ### Supported schemes
diff --git a/src/common.sh b/src/common.sh
index c0a4acc..57a1d70 100644
--- a/src/common.sh
+++ b/src/common.sh
@@ -99,7 +99,7 @@ common::curl() {
     local rv=0 code=0 status=
 
     exec {stdout}>&1
-    { code=$(curl -o "/proc/self/fd/${stdout}" -w '%{http_code}' "$@") || rv=$?; } {stdout}>&1
+    { code=$(curl -o "/proc/self/fd/${stdout}" ${CURL_ERROUT+--stderr "/proc/self/fd/${stdout}"} -w '%{http_code}' "$@") || rv=$?; } {stdout}>&1
     exec {stdout}>&-
 
     if [ "${code}" -ge 400 ] 2> /dev/null; then
diff --git a/src/docker.sh b/src/docker.sh
index c9a3173..a68fe76 100644
--- a/src/docker.sh
+++ b/src/docker.sh
@@ -31,7 +31,7 @@ docker::_authenticate() {
 
     # Query the registry to see if we're authorized.
     common::log INFO "Querying registry for permission grant"
-    resp_headers=$(CURL_IGNORE=401 common::curl "${curl_opts[@]}" -I ${req_params[@]+"${req_params[@]}"} -- "${url}")
+    resp_headers=$(CURL_IGNORE=401 common::curl "${curl_opts[@]}" -I -- "${url}")
 
     # If we don't need to authenticate, we're done.
     if ! grep -qi '^www-authenticate:' <<< "${resp_headers}"; then
@@ -40,11 +40,13 @@ docker::_authenticate() {
     fi
 
     # Otherwise, craft a new token request from the WWW-Authenticate header.
-    printf "%s" "${resp_headers}" | awk -F '="|",' '(tolower($1) ~ "^www-authenticate:"){
+    printf "%s" "${resp_headers}" | awk '(tolower($1) ~ "^www-authenticate:"){
         sub(/"\r/, "", $0)
-        print $2
-        for (i=3; i<=NF; i+=2) print "--data-urlencode\n" $i"="$(i+1)
-    }' | { common::read -r realm; readarray -t req_params; }
+        for (i = 1; i <= split($3, params, /="|",/); i += 2)
+            tolower(params[i]) == "realm" ?  realm = params[i+1] : data[params[i]] = params[i+1]
+
+        print $2; print realm; for (i in data) print "--data-urlencode\n" i"="data[i]
+    }' | { common::read -r auth; common::read -r realm; readarray -t req_params; }
 
     if [ -z "${realm}" ]; then
         common::err "Could not parse authentication realm from ${url}"
@@ -62,17 +64,29 @@ docker::_authenticate() {
         fi
     fi
 
-    # Request a new token.
-    common::curl "${curl_opts[@]}" -G ${req_params[@]+"${req_params[@]}"} -- "${realm}" \
-      | common::jq -r '.token? // .access_token? // empty' \
-      | common::read -r token
+    case "${auth}" in
+    Bearer)
+        # Request a new token.
+        common::curl "${curl_opts[@]}" -G ${req_params[@]+"${req_params[@]}"} -- "${realm}" \
+          | common::jq -r '.token? // .access_token? // empty' \
+          | common::read -r token
+        ;;
+    Basic)
+        # Check that we have valid credentials and save them if successful.
+        CURL_ERROUT=1 common::curl "${curl_opts[@]}" -G -v ${req_params[@]+"${req_params[@]}"} -- "${url}" \
+          | awk '/Authorization: Basic/ { sub(/\r/, "", $4); print $4 }' \
+          | common::read -r token
+        ;;
+    *)
+        common::err "Unsupported authentication method ${auth}" ;;
+    esac
 
     [ -v fd ] && exec {fd}>&-
 
     # Store the new token.
     if [ -n "${token}" ]; then
         mkdir -m 0700 -p "${token_dir}"
-        (umask 077 && printf 'header "Authorization: Bearer %s"' "${token}" > "${token_dir}/${registry}.$$")
+        (umask 077 && printf 'header "Authorization: %s %s"' "${auth}" "${token}" > "${token_dir}/${registry}.$$")
         common::log INFO "Authentication succeeded"
     fi
 }
@@ -111,7 +125,7 @@ docker::_download() {
     local -r user="$1" registry="${2:-registry-1.docker.io}" tag="${4:-latest}" arch="$5"
     local image="$3"
 
-    if  [[ "${image}" != */* ]]; then
+    if  [[ "${image}" != */* ]] && [[ "${registry}" =~ docker\.io$ ]]; then
         image="library/${image}"
     fi
 
@@ -294,7 +308,7 @@ docker::import() (
 
     # Extract all the layers locally.
     common::log INFO "Extracting image layers..." NL
-    parallel --plain ${TTY_ON+--bar} -j "${ENROOT_MAX_PROCESSORS}" mkdir {\#}\; tar -C {\#} --warning=no-timestamp --anchored --exclude='dev/*' \
+    parallel --plain ${TTY_ON+--bar} -j "${ENROOT_MAX_PROCESSORS}" mkdir {\#}\; tar -C {\#} --warning=no-timestamp --anchored --exclude='dev/*' --exclude='./dev/*' \
       --use-compress-program=zstd -pxf \'"${ENROOT_CACHE_PATH}/{}"\' ::: "${layers[@]}"
     common::fixperms .
     common::log

author: Jonathan Calmels
message: Add support for ppc64le
patch:
commit 47ec3cae44bb101e313395b9ff997849ffa29528
Author: Jonathan Calmels <jcalmels@nvidia.com>
Date:   Thu Jan 14 17:48:34 2021 -0800

    Add support for ppc64le
    
    Fixes: #52
---
 Makefile             | 5 +++++
 bin/enroot-nsenter.c | 7 ++++---
 deps/Makefile        | 9 +++++++++
 pkg/debbuild         | 8 ++++++++
 src/common.sh        | 2 ++
 5 files changed, 28 insertions(+), 3 deletions(-)

diff --git a/Makefile b/Makefile
index c036576..71b475d 100644
--- a/Makefile
+++ b/Makefile
@@ -78,6 +78,11 @@ else
 CFLAGS   += -s
 endif
 
+# Required for Musl on PPC64
+ifeq "$(ARCH:power%=p%)" "ppc64le"
+CFLAGS   += -mlong-double-64
+endif
+
 # Infer the compiler used for cross compilation if not specified.
 ifeq "$(origin CC)" "default"
 CC       := $(shell readlink -f $(shell sh -c 'command -v $(CC)'))
diff --git a/bin/enroot-nsenter.c b/bin/enroot-nsenter.c
index fcc0860..59e0c27 100644
--- a/bin/enroot-nsenter.c
+++ b/bin/enroot-nsenter.c
@@ -14,7 +14,7 @@
  * limitations under the License.
  */
 
-#if !defined(__x86_64__) && !defined(__aarch64__)
+#if !defined(__x86_64__) && !defined(__aarch64__) && !defined(__powerpc64__)
 # error "unsupported architecture"
 #endif
 
@@ -93,8 +93,9 @@
 static struct sock_filter filter[] = {
         /* Check for the syscall architecture (x86_64 and aarch64 ABIs). */
         BPF_STMT(BPF_LD|BPF_W|BPF_ABS, offsetof(struct seccomp_data, arch)),
-        BPF_JUMP(BPF_JMP|BPF_JEQ|BPF_K, AUDIT_ARCH_X86_64,  2, 0),
-        BPF_JUMP(BPF_JMP|BPF_JEQ|BPF_K, AUDIT_ARCH_AARCH64, 1, 0),
+        BPF_JUMP(BPF_JMP|BPF_JEQ|BPF_K, AUDIT_ARCH_X86_64,  3, 0),
+        BPF_JUMP(BPF_JMP|BPF_JEQ|BPF_K, AUDIT_ARCH_AARCH64, 2, 0),
+        BPF_JUMP(BPF_JMP|BPF_JEQ|BPF_K, AUDIT_ARCH_PPC64LE, 1, 0),
         /* FIXME We do not support x86, x32 and aarch32, allow all syscalls for now. */
         BPF_STMT(BPF_RET|BPF_K, SECCOMP_RET_ALLOW),
 
diff --git a/deps/Makefile b/deps/Makefile
index d2fc208..3ff944b 100644
--- a/deps/Makefile
+++ b/deps/Makefile
@@ -15,6 +15,11 @@ endif
 export CPPFLAGS := -D_FORTIFY_SOURCE=2
 export CFLAGS   := -O2 -fdata-sections -ffunction-sections -fstack-protector -fPIE
 
+# Required for Musl on PPC64
+ifeq "$(ARCH:power%=p%)" "ppc64le"
+CFLAGS   += -mlong-double-64
+endif
+
 MAKEOVERRIDES :=
 
 makeself/.stamp:
@@ -36,6 +41,10 @@ musl/.stamp:
 	touch $(CURDIR)/dist/musl/include/sys/cdefs.h
 	touch $@
 
+# XXX Quirk for PPC64 directory name
+ifeq "$(ARCH:power%=p%)" "ppc64le"
+linux-headers/.stamp: export ARCH:=powerpc
+endif
 linux-headers/.stamp:
 	$(MAKE) -C linux-headers install prefix=$(CURDIR)/dist/musl
 	touch $@
diff --git a/pkg/debbuild b/pkg/debbuild
index 8138191..04fde94 100755
--- a/pkg/debbuild
+++ b/pkg/debbuild
@@ -31,6 +31,14 @@ rc() {
 
         export ARCH=aarch64
         export CC=aarch64-linux-gnu-gcc-8
+    elif [[ "${CROSS_COMPILE}" =~ "powerpc64le-linux-gnu" ]]; then
+        apt-get -y install --no-install-recommends \
+            gcc-8-powerpc64le-linux-gnu \
+            linux-libc-dev-ppc64el-cross \
+            libc6-dev-ppc64el-cross
+
+        export ARCH=powerpc64le
+        export CC=powerpc64le-linux-gnu-gcc-8
     fi
 
     cd /usr/local/src/enroot
diff --git a/src/common.sh b/src/common.sh
index 8137411..c0a4acc 100644
--- a/src/common.sh
+++ b/src/common.sh
@@ -257,6 +257,8 @@ common::debarch() {
         printf "amd64" ;;
     aarch64)
         printf "arm64" ;;
+    ppc64le)
+        printf "ppc64le" ;;
     *)
         common::err "Unsupported architecture: ${arch}" ;;
     esac

author: Jonathan Calmels
message: Add support for management devices in Mellanox hook
patch:
commit 15f94047fb2fa9de38b7c07dd95f421d2068f760
Author: Jonathan Calmels <jcalmels@nvidia.com>
Date:   Sun Apr 4 02:14:51 2021 -0700

    Add support for management devices in Mellanox hook
---
 conf/hooks/99-mellanox.sh | 18 ++++++++++++++++++
 1 file changed, 18 insertions(+)

diff --git a/conf/hooks/99-mellanox.sh b/conf/hooks/99-mellanox.sh
index 21791ca..a8ea354 100755
--- a/conf/hooks/99-mellanox.sh
+++ b/conf/hooks/99-mellanox.sh
@@ -23,6 +23,8 @@ fi
 declare -a drivers=()
 declare -a devices=()
 declare -a ifaces=()
+declare -a issms=()
+declare -a umads=()
 declare -A providers=()
 
 # Lookup all the devices and their respective driver.
@@ -40,6 +42,15 @@ for uevent in /sys/bus/pci/drivers/mlx?_core/*/infiniband/*/uevent; do
     ifaces+=("$(. "${uevent}"; echo "${NAME}")")
 done
 
+# Lookup all the management devices.
+for uevent in /sys/bus/pci/drivers/mlx?_core/*/infiniband_mad/*/uevent; do
+    case "${uevent}" in
+    *issm*) issms+=("$(. "${uevent}"; echo "/dev/${DEVNAME}")") ;;
+    *umad*) umads+=("$(. "${uevent}"; echo "/dev/${DEVNAME}")") ;;
+    *) continue ;;
+    esac
+done
+
 # Hide all the device entries in sysfs by default and mount RDMA CM.
 cat << EOF | enroot-mount --root "${ENROOT_ROOTFS}" -
 tmpfs /sys/class/infiniband tmpfs nosuid,noexec,nodev,mode=755,private
@@ -64,6 +75,13 @@ for id in ${MELLANOX_VISIBLE_DEVICES//,/ }; do
     enroot-mount --root "${ENROOT_ROOTFS}" - <<< "${devices[id]} ${devices[id]} none x-create=file,bind,ro,nosuid,noexec,private"
     ln -s "$(common::realpath "/sys/class/infiniband/${ifaces[id]}")" "${ENROOT_ROOTFS}/sys/class/infiniband/${ifaces[id]}"
     ln -s "$(common::realpath "/sys/class/infiniband_verbs/${devices[id]##*/}")" "${ENROOT_ROOTFS}/sys/class/infiniband_verbs/${devices[id]##*/}"
+
+    if [ -n "${ENROOT_ALLOW_SUPERUSER-}" ] && [ "$(awk '{print $2}' /proc/self/uid_map)" -eq 0 ]; then
+        enroot-mount --root "${ENROOT_ROOTFS}" - <<< "${umads[id]} ${umads[id]} none x-create=file,bind,ro,nosuid,noexec,private,nofail,silent"
+        enroot-mount --root "${ENROOT_ROOTFS}" - <<< "${issms[id]} ${issms[id]} none x-create=file,bind,ro,nosuid,noexec,private,nofail,silent"
+        ln -s "$(common::realpath "/sys/class/infiniband_mad/${umads[id]##*/}")" "${ENROOT_ROOTFS}/sys/class/infiniband_mad/${umads[id]##*/}"
+        ln -s "$(common::realpath "/sys/class/infiniband_mad/${issms[id]##*/}")" "${ENROOT_ROOTFS}/sys/class/infiniband_mad/${issms[id]##*/}"
+    fi
 done
 
 if [ -z "${MELLANOX_MOUNT_DRIVER-}" ]; then

author: Jonathan Calmels

author: Jonathan Calmels
message: Add new hook to suppress apt warning about the sandbox user
patch:
commit 3d9bb215eb6ee6d72a687116090a1de6f52b700a
Author: Jonathan Calmels <jcalmels@nvidia.com>
Date:   Sat Apr 3 17:59:56 2021 -0700

    Add new hook to suppress apt warning about the sandbox user
---
 Makefile                |  3 ++-
 conf/hooks/10-aptfix.sh | 26 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 2 files changed, 28 insertions(+), 1 deletion(-)

diff --git a/Makefile b/Makefile
index 208cae4..c036576 100644
--- a/Makefile
+++ b/Makefile
@@ -40,7 +40,8 @@ UTILS := bin/enroot-aufs2ovlfs    \
 
 CONFIG := conf/enroot.conf
 
-HOOKS := conf/hooks/10-cgroups.sh  \
+HOOKS := conf/hooks/10-aptfix.sh   \
+         conf/hooks/10-cgroups.sh  \
          conf/hooks/10-devices.sh  \
          conf/hooks/10-home.sh     \
          conf/hooks/10-shadow.sh   \
diff --git a/conf/hooks/10-aptfix.sh b/conf/hooks/10-aptfix.sh
new file mode 100755
index 0000000..4b0cb61
--- /dev/null
+++ b/conf/hooks/10-aptfix.sh
@@ -0,0 +1,26 @@
+#! /usr/bin/env bash
+
+# Copyright (c) 2018-2020, NVIDIA CORPORATION. All rights reserved.
+#
+# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
+# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
+# You may obtain a copy of the License at
+#
+#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
+#
+# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
+# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
+# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
+# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
+# limitations under the License.
+
+set -eu
+
+if [ ! -f "${ENROOT_ROOTFS}/etc/debian_version" ]; then
+    exit 0
+fi
+
+if [ -n "${ENROOT_REMAP_ROOT-}" ]; then
+    mkdir -p "${ENROOT_ROOTFS}/etc/apt/apt.conf.d"
+    printf 'APT::Sandbox::User "root";\n' > "${ENROOT_ROOTFS}/etc/apt/apt.conf.d/enroot"
+fi

author: Jonathan Calmels
message: Add Oracle Linux 7 to the list of bundle checks
patch:
commit c46db30302c7fcb188dd96b441959f70cb92b8c7
Author: Jonathan Calmels <jcalmels@nvidia.com>
Date:   Sat Apr 3 17:32:46 2021 -0700

    Add Oracle Linux 7 to the list of bundle checks
---

diff --git a/src/bundle.sh b/src/bundle.sh
index 14386d8..70e271e 100644
--- a/src/bundle.sh
+++ b/src/bundle.sh
@@ -145,7 +145,7 @@ bundle::verify() {
 
     printf "\n%s\n\n" "$(common::fmt bold "Kernel command line:")"
     case "$(. /etc/os-release 2> /dev/null; echo "${ID-}${VERSION_ID-}")" in
-    centos7*|rhel7*)
+    centos7*|rhel7*|ol7*)
         for param in "namespace.unpriv_enable=1" "user_namespace.enable=1"; do
             if grep -q "${param}" /proc/cmdline; then
                 printf "%-34s: %s\n" "${param}" "$(common::fmt green "OK")"

author: Tobias Pape
message: [rpm] fix date in spec file to silence rpmbuild
patch:
commit d133e239170fe92d3288fb6ebd9d85ba4fb82ef5
Author: Tobias Pape <tobias@netshed.de>
Date:   Thu Jan 14 14:33:58 2021 +0100

    [rpm] fix date in spec file to silence rpmbuild
    
    rpmbuild complained that 2019-10-30 was no thursday:
    
    ```
    RPM build errors:
        bogus date in %changelog: Thu Oct 30 2019 NVIDIA CORPORATION <cudatools@nvidia.com> 2.2.0-1
    ```
    
    Signed-off-by: Tobias Pape <tobias@netshed.de>
---
 pkg/rpm/SPECS/enroot.spec | 2 +-
 1 file changed, 1 insertion(+), 1 deletion(-)

diff --git a/pkg/rpm/SPECS/enroot.spec b/pkg/rpm/SPECS/enroot.spec
index 1a10317..6c2af70 100644
--- a/pkg/rpm/SPECS/enroot.spec
+++ b/pkg/rpm/SPECS/enroot.spec
@@ -76,7 +76,7 @@ setcap cap_sys_admin,cap_mknod-pe "$(command -v enroot-aufs2ovlfs)"
 * Sat Feb 01 2020 %{packager} 3.0.0-1
 - Release v3.0.0
 
-* Thu Oct 30 2019 %{packager} 2.2.0-1
+* Wed Oct 30 2019 %{packager} 2.2.0-1
 - Release v2.2.0
 
 * Thu Oct 17 2019 %{packager} 2.1.0-1

author: Jonathan Calmels
message: Add extra hook for MIG config
patch:
commit 6c44fd6d0d46bd69a7392b696e20df6a23b45a4f
Author: Jonathan Calmels <jcalmels@nvidia.com>
Date:   Wed Jan 13 13:20:44 2021 -0800

    Add extra hook for MIG config
---
 conf/hooks/extra/50-mig-config.sh | 38 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 1 file changed, 38 insertions(+)

diff --git a/conf/hooks/extra/50-mig-config.sh b/conf/hooks/extra/50-mig-config.sh
new file mode 100755
index 0000000..8e229c4
--- /dev/null
+++ b/conf/hooks/extra/50-mig-config.sh
@@ -0,0 +1,38 @@
+#! /usr/bin/env bash
+
+# Copyright (c) 2018-2020, NVIDIA CORPORATION. All rights reserved.
+#
+# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
+# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
+# You may obtain a copy of the License at
+#
+#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
+#
+# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
+# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
+# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
+# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
+# limitations under the License.
+
+set -euo pipefail
+
+source "${ENROOT_LIBRARY_PATH}/common.sh"
+
+common::checkcmd grep nvidia-smi
+
+if [ "${NVIDIA_VISIBLE_DEVICES:-void}" = "void" ] || [ "${NVIDIA_VISIBLE_DEVICES}" = "none" ]; then
+    exit 0
+fi
+if [[ "${NVIDIA_VISIBLE_DEVICES}" =~ "MIG-" ]] || [[ "${NVIDIA_VISIBLE_DEVICES}" =~ .:. ]]; then
+    exit 0
+fi
+
+nvsmi_args=("--query-gpu=mig.mode.current" "--format=csv,noheader")
+
+if [ "${NVIDIA_VISIBLE_DEVICES}" != "all" ]; then
+    nvsmi_args+=("-i" "${NVIDIA_VISIBLE_DEVICES}")
+fi
+
+if nvidia-smi "${nvsmi_args[@]}" | grep -q "Enabled"; then
+    echo "NVIDIA_MIG_CONFIG_DEVICES=all" >> "${ENROOT_ENVIRON}"
+fi

author: Jonathan Calmels
message: Add MIG support to NVIDIA hook
patch:
commit c3922846cb18ebe614d1c845315e801b3006c4a6
Author: Jonathan Calmels <jcalmels@nvidia.com>
Date:   Tue Dec 1 22:56:58 2020 -0800

    Add MIG support to NVIDIA hook
---
 conf/hooks/98-nvidia.sh | 9 +++++++++
 1 file changed, 9 insertions(+)

diff --git a/conf/hooks/98-nvidia.sh b/conf/hooks/98-nvidia.sh
index de2b814..6fcd393 100755
--- a/conf/hooks/98-nvidia.sh
+++ b/conf/hooks/98-nvidia.sh
@@ -37,6 +37,15 @@ if [ "${NVIDIA_VISIBLE_DEVICES}" != "none" ]; then
     cli_args+=("--device=${NVIDIA_VISIBLE_DEVICES}")
 fi
 
+# https://github.com/NVIDIA/nvidia-container-runtime#nvidia_mig_config_devices
+if [ -n "${NVIDIA_MIG_CONFIG_DEVICES-}" ]; then
+    cli_args+=("--mig-config=${NVIDIA_MIG_CONFIG_DEVICES}")
+fi
+# https://github.com/NVIDIA/nvidia-container-runtime#nvidia_mig_monitor_devices
+if [ -n "${NVIDIA_MIG_MONITOR_DEVICES-}" ]; then
+    cli_args+=("--mig-monitor=${NVIDIA_MIG_MONITOR_DEVICES}")
+fi
+
 # https://github.com/nvidia/nvidia-container-runtime#nvidia_driver_capabilities
 if [ -z "${NVIDIA_DRIVER_CAPABILITIES-}" ]; then
     NVIDIA_DRIVER_CAPABILITIES="utility"

author: Jonathan Calmels
message: Fix seccomp filter for 32-bit binaries
patch:
commit 77bb892ff1738fd65c2dfc437f82fb7d57888c0b
Author: Jonathan Calmels <jcalmels@nvidia.com>
Date:   Tue Nov 10 01:04:05 2020 -0800

    Fix seccomp filter for 32-bit binaries
    
    Fixes: #43
---
 bin/enroot-nsenter.c | 5 +++--
 1 file changed, 3 insertions(+), 2 deletions(-)

diff --git a/bin/enroot-nsenter.c b/bin/enroot-nsenter.c
index 21110df..77b9daf 100644
--- a/bin/enroot-nsenter.c
+++ b/bin/enroot-nsenter.c
@@ -91,11 +91,12 @@
 #endif
 
 static struct sock_filter filter[] = {
-        /* Check for the syscall architecture (x86_64/x32 and aarch64 ABIs). */
+        /* Check for the syscall architecture (x86_64 and aarch64 ABIs). */
         BPF_STMT(BPF_LD|BPF_W|BPF_ABS, offsetof(struct seccomp_data, arch)),
         BPF_JUMP(BPF_JMP|BPF_JEQ|BPF_K, AUDIT_ARCH_X86_64,  2, 0),
         BPF_JUMP(BPF_JMP|BPF_JEQ|BPF_K, AUDIT_ARCH_AARCH64, 1, 0),
-        BPF_STMT(BPF_RET|BPF_K, SECCOMP_RET_KILL),
+        /* FIXME We do not support x86, x32 and aarch32, allow all syscalls for now. */
+        BPF_STMT(BPF_RET|BPF_K, SECCOMP_RET_ALLOW),
 
         /* Load the syscall number. */
         BPF_STMT(BPF_LD|BPF_W|BPF_ABS, offsetof(struct seccomp_data, nr)),

patch:

diff --git a/enroot.in b/enroot.in

-    case "${1-}" in
+    case "$1" in
     batch)
         cat <<- EOF
 		Usage: ${0##*/} batch [options] [--] CONFIG [COMMAND] [ARG...]
@@ -210,7 +210,7 @@ enroot::usage() {
 		   -m, --mount FSTAB    Perform a mount from the host inside the container (colon-separated)
 		EOF
         ;;
-    ""|*)
+    help|*)
         cat <<- EOF
 		Usage: ${0##*/} COMMAND [ARG...]
 		
@@ -230,7 +230,7 @@ enroot::usage() {
 		EOF
         ;;
     esac
-    exit 0
+    exit "$2"
 }

@@ -243,35 +243,37 @@ enroot::import() {
     while [ $# -gt 0 ]; do
         case "$1" in
         -a|--arch)
-            [ -z "${2-}" ] && enroot::usage import
+            [ -z "${2-}" ] && enroot::usage import 1
             arch="$2"
             shift 2
             ;;
         --arch=*)
-           [ -z "${1#*=}" ] && enroot::usage import

            arch="${1#*=}"
            shift
            ;;

-            [ -z "${2-}" ] && enroot::usage import

             filename="$2"

-           [ -z "${1#*=}" ] && enroot::usage import

            filename="${1#*=}"
            shift
            ;;
+        -h|--help)
+            enroot::usage import 0 ;;
         --)
             shift; break ;;
         -?*)
-            enroot::usage import ;;
+            enroot::usage import 1 ;;
         *)
             break ;;
         esac
     done
     if [ $# -lt 1 ]; then
-        enroot::usage import
+        enroot::usage import 1
     fi
     uri="$1"
 
@@ -288,25 +290,27 @@ enroot::export() {
             shift
             ;;
         -o|--output)

             shift 2
             ;;

-            [ -z "${1#*=}" ] && enroot::usage export
+            [ -z "${1#*=}" ] && enroot::usage export 1
             filename="${1#*=}"
             shift
             ;;
+        -h|--help)
+            enroot::usage export 0 ;;

         -?*)
-            enroot::usage export ;;
+            enroot::usage export 1 ;;
         *)
             break ;;
         esac
     done
     if [ $# -lt 1 ]; then
-        enroot::usage export
+        enroot::usage export 1
     fi
     name="$1"
 
@@ -323,25 +327,27 @@ enroot::create() {
             shift
             ;;
         -n|--name)
-            [ -z "${2-}" ] && enroot::usage create
+            [ -z "${2-}" ] && enroot::usage create 1

-            [ -z "${1#*=}" ] && enroot::usage create

             ;;
+        -h|--help)
+            enroot::usage create 0 ;;
         --)
             shift; break ;;

-            enroot::usage create ;;
+            enroot::usage create 1 ;;
         *)
             break ;;
         esac
     done
     if [ $# -lt 1 ]; then
-        enroot::usage create
+        enroot::usage create 1
     fi
     image="$1"
 
@@ -354,32 +360,32 @@ enroot::start() {
     while [ $# -gt 0 ]; do
         case "$1" in
         -c|--conf)
-            [ -z "${2-}" ] && enroot::usage start
+            [ -z "${2-}" ] && enroot::usage start 1
             conf="$2"
             shift 2
             ;;
         --conf=*)
-            [ -z "${1#*=}" ] && enroot::usage start
+            [ -z "${1#*=}" ] && enroot::usage start 1
             conf="${1#*=}"

             ;;
         -m|--mount)
-            [ -z "${2-}" ] && enroot::usage start

             mounts+=("$2")

             ;;
         --mount=*)

             mounts+=("${1#*=}")

             environ+=("$2")

             ;;
         --env=*)
-            [ -z "${1#*=}" ] && enroot::usage start
+            [ -z "${1#*=}" ] && enroot::usage start 1
             environ+=("${1#*=}")
             shift
             ;;
@@ -388,12 +394,12 @@ enroot::start() {
             shift
             ;;
         --rc)
-            [ -z "${2-}" ] && enroot::usage start
+            [ -z "${2-}" ] && enroot::usage start 1
             rc="$2"

-            [ -z "${1#*=}" ] && enroot::usage start

             rc="${1#*=}"

@@ -401,16 +407,18 @@ enroot::start() {

             break ;;
         esac
     done

     fi

@@ -439,25 +447,27 @@ enroot::exec() {

     if [ $# -lt 2 ]; then

     fi
     pid="$1"
     shift
@@ -472,16 +482,18 @@ enroot::batch() {
 
     while [ $# -gt 0 ]; do

author: Jonathan Calmels
message: Add COMM to enroot list -f
patch:
commit b7e74720794fbb8ff45c49ecc392623de75ab870
Author: Jonathan Calmels <jcalmels@nvidia.com>
Date:   Mon Nov 9 23:01:36 2020 -0800

    Add COMM to enroot list -f
    
    Fixes: #33
---
 src/runtime.sh | 6 ++++--
 1 file changed, 4 insertions(+), 2 deletions(-)

diff --git a/src/runtime.sh b/src/runtime.sh
index 6cabe2d..fe57dbc 100644
--- a/src/runtime.sh
+++ b/src/runtime.sh
@@ -524,7 +524,7 @@ runtime::list() {
 
     # List all the rootfs entries and their respective processes.
     {
-        printf "NAME\tPID\tSTATE\tSTARTED\tTIME\tMNTNS\tUSERNS\tCOMMAND\n"
+        printf "NAME\tPID\tCOMM\tSTATE\tSTARTED\tTIME\tMNTNS\tUSERNS\tCOMMAND\n"
         for name in $(printf "%s\n" "${!info[@]}" | sort); do
             entry=(${info["${name}"]})
             if [ "${#entry[@]}" -eq 0 ]; then
@@ -532,9 +532,11 @@ runtime::list() {
             else
                 ps -p "${entry[*]}" --no-headers -o pid:1,stat:1,stime:1,etime:1,mntns:1,userns:1,command:1 \
                   | awk -v name="${name}" '{
+                      getline comm < ("/proc/"$1"/comm")
                       printf (NR==1) ? "%s\t" : " \t", name
-                      printf "%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\t", $1, $2, $3, $4, $5, $6
+                      printf "%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\t", $1, comm, $2, $3, $4, $5, $6
                       print substr($0, index($0, $7))
+                      close("/proc/"$1"/comm")
                   }'
             fi
         done

author: Jonathan Calmels
message: Make some mounts non-fatal in hooks
patch:
commit d80b3f6749bd796d05c136bb30788e7acd63afd6
Author: Jonathan Calmels <jcalmels@nvidia.com>
Date:   Wed Aug 5 17:27:08 2020 -0700

    Make some mounts non-fatal in hooks
---
 conf/hooks/99-mellanox.sh        | 4 ++--
 conf/hooks/extra/50-slurm-pmi.sh | 4 ++--
 2 files changed, 4 insertions(+), 4 deletions(-)

diff --git a/conf/hooks/99-mellanox.sh b/conf/hooks/99-mellanox.sh
index 4d13a85..21791ca 100755
--- a/conf/hooks/99-mellanox.sh
+++ b/conf/hooks/99-mellanox.sh
@@ -44,10 +44,10 @@ done
 cat << EOF | enroot-mount --root "${ENROOT_ROOTFS}" -
 tmpfs /sys/class/infiniband tmpfs nosuid,noexec,nodev,mode=755,private
 tmpfs /sys/class/infiniband_verbs tmpfs nosuid,noexec,nodev,mode=755,private
-tmpfs /sys/class/infiniband_cm tmpfs nosuid,noexec,nodev,mode=755,private
+tmpfs /sys/class/infiniband_cm tmpfs nosuid,noexec,nodev,mode=755,private,nofail,silent
 tmpfs /sys/class/infiniband_mad tmpfs nosuid,noexec,nodev,mode=755,private
 /sys/class/infiniband_verbs/abi_version /sys/class/infiniband_verbs/abi_version none x-create=file,bind,ro,nosuid,noexec,nodev,private
-/sys/class/infiniband_cm/abi_version /sys/class/infiniband_cm/abi_version none x-create=file,bind,ro,nosuid,noexec,nodev,private
+/sys/class/infiniband_cm/abi_version /sys/class/infiniband_cm/abi_version none x-create=file,bind,ro,nosuid,noexec,nodev,private,nofail,silent
 /sys/class/infiniband_mad/abi_version /sys/class/infiniband_mad/abi_version none x-create=file,bind,ro,nosuid,noexec,nodev,private
 /dev/infiniband/rdma_cm /dev/infiniband/rdma_cm none x-create=file,bind,ro,nosuid,noexec,private
 EOF
diff --git a/conf/hooks/extra/50-slurm-pmi.sh b/conf/hooks/extra/50-slurm-pmi.sh
index ab83350..51ccb08 100755
--- a/conf/hooks/extra/50-slurm-pmi.sh
+++ b/conf/hooks/extra/50-slurm-pmi.sh
@@ -52,9 +52,9 @@ if [[ -z "${SLURM_MPI_TYPE-}" || "${SLURM_MPI_TYPE}" == pmix* ]] && compgen -e "
     fi
 
     if [ -e "${slurm_tmpfs}/spmix_appdir_${SLURM_JOB_UID}_${SLURM_JOB_ID}.${SLURM_STEP_ID}" ]; then
-        printf "%s x-create=dir,bind,rw,nosuid,noexec,nodev,private\n" "${slurm_tmpfs}/spmix_appdir_${SLURM_JOB_UID}_${SLURM_JOB_ID}.${SLURM_STEP_ID}" >> "${ENROOT_MOUNTS}"
+        printf "%s x-create=dir,bind,rw,nosuid,noexec,nodev,private,nofail\n" "${slurm_tmpfs}/spmix_appdir_${SLURM_JOB_UID}_${SLURM_JOB_ID}.${SLURM_STEP_ID}" >> "${ENROOT_MOUNTS}"
     else
-        printf "%s x-create=dir,bind,rw,nosuid,noexec,nodev,private\n" "${slurm_tmpfs}/spmix_appdir_${SLURM_JOB_ID}.${SLURM_STEP_ID}" >> "${ENROOT_MOUNTS}"
+        printf "%s x-create=dir,bind,rw,nosuid,noexec,nodev,private,nofail\n" "${slurm_tmpfs}/spmix_appdir_${SLURM_JOB_ID}.${SLURM_STEP_ID}" >> "${ENROOT_MOUNTS}"
     fi
     printf "%s x-create=dir,bind,rw,nosuid,noexec,nodev,private\n" "${slurm_spool}/pmix.${SLURM_JOB_ID}.${SLURM_STEP_ID}" >> "${ENROOT_MOUNTS}"
 fi

author: Jonathan Calmels
message: Use SLURM_STEP_NODELIST in Pytorch hook in case of node reordering
patch:
commit 6ac4afc6218c11768f6a5167411a7437a5873edc
Author: Jonathan Calmels <jcalmels@nvidia.com>
Date:   Thu Jul 30 01:53:31 2020 -0700

    Use SLURM_STEP_NODELIST in Pytorch hook in case of node reordering
---
 conf/hooks/extra/50-slurm-pytorch.sh | 4 ++--
 1 file changed, 2 insertions(+), 2 deletions(-)

diff --git a/conf/hooks/extra/50-slurm-pytorch.sh b/conf/hooks/extra/50-slurm-pytorch.sh
index 12f596b..28c0fe0 100755
--- a/conf/hooks/extra/50-slurm-pytorch.sh
+++ b/conf/hooks/extra/50-slurm-pytorch.sh
@@ -20,8 +20,8 @@ if ! grep -q "^PYTORCH_VERSION=" "${ENROOT_ENVIRON}"; then
     exit 0
 fi
 
-if [ -n "${SLURM_NODELIST-}" ] && ! grep -q "^MASTER_ADDR=" "${ENROOT_ENVIRON}" && command -v scontrol > /dev/null; then
-    printf "MASTER_ADDR=%s\n" "$(scontrol show hostname "${SLURM_NODELIST}" | head -n1)" >> "${ENROOT_ENVIRON}"
+if [ -n "${SLURM_STEP_NODELIST-}" ] && ! grep -q "^MASTER_ADDR=" "${ENROOT_ENVIRON}" && command -v scontrol > /dev/null; then
+    printf "MASTER_ADDR=%s\n" "$(scontrol show hostname "${SLURM_STEP_NODELIST}" | head -n1)" >> "${ENROOT_ENVIRON}"
 fi
 if [ -n "${SLURM_JOB_ID-}" ] && ! grep -q "^MASTER_PORT=" "${ENROOT_ENVIRON}"; then
     printf "MASTER_PORT=%s\n" "$((${SLURM_JOB_ID} % 16384 + 49152))" >> "${ENROOT_ENVIRON}"

 fi

author: Jonathan Calmels
message: Add more force overrides with ENROOT_FORCE_OVERRIDE
patch:
commit 393ba4136f522058022332fd8a734f27908fd4ba
Author: Jonathan Calmels <jcalmels@nvidia.com>
Date:   Tue Jun 30 14:07:51 2020 -0700

    Add more force overrides with ENROOT_FORCE_OVERRIDE
---
 conf/enroot.conf.in |  3 +++
 doc/cmd/bundle.md   |  2 ++
 doc/cmd/create.md   |  9 ++++++++-
 doc/cmd/export.md   |  2 ++
 doc/cmd/remove.md   |  6 ++++++
 enroot.in           | 24 ++++++++++++++++++++----
 src/runtime.sh      | 24 ++++++++++++++++++------
 7 files changed, 59 insertions(+), 11 deletions(-)

diff --git a/conf/enroot.conf.in b/conf/enroot.conf.in
index eb67d2b..84f36c5 100644
--- a/conf/enroot.conf.in
+++ b/conf/enroot.conf.in
@@ -57,6 +57,9 @@
 # Restrict /dev inside the container to a minimal set of devices.
 #ENROOT_RESTRICT_DEV        no
 
+# Always use --force on command invocations.
+#ENROOT_FORCE_OVERRIDE      no
+
 # SSL certificates settings:
 #SSL_CERT_DIR
 #SSL_CERT_FILE
diff --git a/doc/cmd/bundle.md b/doc/cmd/bundle.md
index 4e7417f..f894ce3 100644
--- a/doc/cmd/bundle.md
+++ b/doc/cmd/bundle.md
@@ -10,6 +10,7 @@ Create a self-extracting bundle from a container image.
    -d, --desc TEXT      Provide a description of the bundle
    -o, --output BUNDLE  Name of the output bundle file (defaults to "IMAGE.run")
    -t, --target DIR     Target directory used by --keep (defaults to "$PWD/BUNDLE")
+   -f, --force          Overwrite an existing bundle
 ```
 ```
 Usage: bundle.run [options] [--] [COMMAND] [ARG...]
@@ -47,6 +48,7 @@ If `--keep` is not provided at launch, `$ENROOT_TEMP_PATH` will be used for extr
 | ------ | ------ | ------ |
 | `ENROOT_BUNDLE_ALL` | `no` | Include runtime and user-specific configuration inside bundles (same as `--all`) |
 | `ENROOT_BUNDLE_CHECKSUM` | `no` | Generate an embedded checksum inside bundles (same as `--checksum`) |
+| `ENROOT_FORCE_OVERRIDE` | `no` | Overwrite the bundle if it already exists (same as `--force`) |
 
 # Example
 
diff --git a/doc/cmd/create.md b/doc/cmd/create.md
index 36b4711..f842df4 100644
--- a/doc/cmd/create.md
+++ b/doc/cmd/create.md
@@ -6,7 +6,8 @@ Usage: enroot create [options] [--] IMAGE
 Create a container root filesystem from a container image.
 
  Options:
-   -n, --name  Name of the container (defaults to "IMAGE")
+   -n, --name   Name of the container (defaults to "IMAGE")
+   -f, --force  Overwrite an existing root filesystem
    ```
    
 # Description
@@ -14,6 +15,12 @@ Create a container root filesystem from a container image.
 Take a container image and unpack its root filesystem under `$ENROOT_DATA_PATH/`.  
 The resulting root filesystem can be started with the [start](start.md) command or removed with the [remove](remove.md) command.
 
+# Configuration
+
+| Setting | Default | Description |
+| ------ | ------ | ------ |
+| `ENROOT_FORCE_OVERRIDE` | `no` | Overwrite the root filesystem if it already exists (same as `--force`) |
+
 # Example
 
 ```sh
diff --git a/doc/cmd/export.md b/doc/cmd/export.md
index ee37b96..79ed680 100644
--- a/doc/cmd/export.md
+++ b/doc/cmd/export.md

author: Jonathan Calmels
message: Add more IB sysfs entries and mount rdma_cm device node
patch:
commit 14d95f341f9614e8776200ee70c6ecda90f34808
Author: Jonathan Calmels <jcalmels@nvidia.com>
Date:   Thu Jun 11 02:26:08 2020 -0700

    Add more IB sysfs entries and mount rdma_cm device node
---
 conf/hooks/99-mellanox.sh | 7 ++++++-
 1 file changed, 6 insertions(+), 1 deletion(-)

diff --git a/conf/hooks/99-mellanox.sh b/conf/hooks/99-mellanox.sh
index bfd4d31..4d13a85 100755
--- a/conf/hooks/99-mellanox.sh
+++ b/conf/hooks/99-mellanox.sh
@@ -40,11 +40,16 @@ for uevent in /sys/bus/pci/drivers/mlx?_core/*/infiniband/*/uevent; do
     ifaces+=("$(. "${uevent}"; echo "${NAME}")")
 done
 
-# Hide all the device entries in sysfs by default.
+# Hide all the device entries in sysfs by default and mount RDMA CM.
 cat << EOF | enroot-mount --root "${ENROOT_ROOTFS}" -
 tmpfs /sys/class/infiniband tmpfs nosuid,noexec,nodev,mode=755,private
 tmpfs /sys/class/infiniband_verbs tmpfs nosuid,noexec,nodev,mode=755,private
+tmpfs /sys/class/infiniband_cm tmpfs nosuid,noexec,nodev,mode=755,private
+tmpfs /sys/class/infiniband_mad tmpfs nosuid,noexec,nodev,mode=755,private
 /sys/class/infiniband_verbs/abi_version /sys/class/infiniband_verbs/abi_version none x-create=file,bind,ro,nosuid,noexec,nodev,private
+/sys/class/infiniband_cm/abi_version /sys/class/infiniband_cm/abi_version none x-create=file,bind,ro,nosuid,noexec,nodev,private
+/sys/class/infiniband_mad/abi_version /sys/class/infiniband_mad/abi_version none x-create=file,bind,ro,nosuid,noexec,nodev,private
+/dev/infiniband/rdma_cm /dev/infiniband/rdma_cm none x-create=file,bind,ro,nosuid,noexec,private
 EOF
 
 # Mount all the visible devices specified.

message: Use a generic error when jq fails to parse its input

     return ${rv}
 }
 
+common::jq() {

+}
+
 common::realpath() {
     local -r path="$1"

diff --git a/src/docker.sh b/src/docker.sh

     # Request a new token.

-      | jq -r '.token? // .access_token? // empty' \

       | common::read -r token
 
     [ -v fd ] && exec {fd}>&-
@@ -129,7 +129,7 @@ docker::_download() {

     CURL_IGNORE="401 404" common::curl "${curl_opts[@]}" "${req_params[@]/manifest/manifest.list}" -- "${url_manifest}" \
-      | jq -r "(.manifests[] | select(.platform.architecture == \"${arch}\") | .digest)? // empty" \
+      | common::jq -r "(.manifests[] | select(.platform.architecture == \"${arch}\") | .digest)? // empty" \
       | common::read -r manifest
 
     if [ -n "${manifest}" ]; then
@@ -139,7 +139,7 @@ docker::_download() {
     # Fetch the image manifest.
     common::log INFO "Fetching image manifest"
     common::curl "${curl_opts[@]}" "${req_params[@]}" -- "${url_manifest}" \
-      | jq -r '(.config.digest | ltrimstr("sha256:"))? // empty, ([.layers[].digest | ltrimstr("sha256:")] | reverse | @tsv)?' \
+      | common::jq -r '(.config.digest | ltrimstr("sha256:"))? // empty, ([.layers[].digest | ltrimstr("sha256:")] | reverse | @tsv)?' \
       | { common::read -r config; IFS=$'\t' common::read -r -a layers; }
 
     if [ -z "${config}" ] || [ "${#layers[@]}" -eq 0 ]; then
@@ -183,26 +183,26 @@ docker::configure() {
 
     if [ -n "${arch}" ]; then
         # Check if the config architecture matches what we expect.
-        jq -r '(.architecture // .Architecture)? // empty' "${config}" | common::read -r platform
+        common::jq -r '(.architecture // .Architecture)? // empty' "${config}" | common::read -r platform
         if [ "${arch}" != "${platform}" ]; then
             common::log WARN "Image architecture doesn't match the requested one: ${platform} != ${arch}"
         fi
     fi
 
     # Configure volumes as simple rootfs bind mounts.
-    jq -r '(.config.Volumes)? // empty | keys[] | "${ENROOT_ROOTFS}\(.) \(.) none x-create=dir,bind,rw,nosuid,nodev"' "${config}" > "${fstab}"
+    common::jq -r '(.config.Volumes)? // empty | keys[] | "${ENROOT_ROOTFS}\(.) \(.) none x-create=dir,bind,rw,nosuid,nodev"' "${config}" > "${fstab}"
 
     # Configure environment variables.
-    jq -r '(.config.Env[])? // empty' "${config}" > "${environ}"
+    common::jq -r '(.config.Env[])? // empty' "${config}" > "${environ}"
 
     # Configure labels as comments.
-    jq -r '(.config.Labels)? // empty | to_entries[] | "# \(.key) \(.value)"' "${config}" > "${initrc}"
+    common::jq -r '(.config.Labels)? // empty | to_entries[] | "# \(.key) \(.value)"' "${config}" > "${initrc}"
     [ -s "${initrc}" ] && echo >> "${initrc}"
 
     # Generate the rc script with the working directory, the entrypoint and the command.
-    jq -r '(.config.WorkingDir)? // empty' "${config}" | common::read -r workdir
-    jq -r '(.config.Entrypoint[])? // empty' "${config}" | readarray -t entrypoint
-    jq -r '(.config.Cmd[])? // empty' "${config}" | readarray -t cmd
+    common::jq -r '(.config.WorkingDir)? // empty' "${config}" | common::read -r workdir
+    common::jq -r '(.config.Entrypoint[])? // empty' "${config}" | readarray -t entrypoint
+    common::jq -r '(.config.Cmd[])? // empty' "${config}" | readarray -t cmd
     if [ "${#entrypoint[@]}" -eq 0 ] && [ "${#cmd[@]}" -eq 0 ]; then
         cmd=("/bin/sh")
     fi
@@ -365,7 +365,7 @@ docker::daemon::import() (
     mkdir rootfs
     docker export "${PWD##*/}" | tar -C rootfs --warning=no-timestamp --anchored --exclude='dev/*' --exclude='.dockerenv' -px
     common::fixperms rootfs
-    docker inspect "${image}" | jq '.[] | with_entries(.key|=ascii_downcase)' > config
+    docker inspect "${image}" | common::jq '.[] | with_entries(.key|=ascii_downcase)' > config
     docker::configure rootfs config "${arch}"
 
     # Create the final squashfs filesystem.

index 7b1f1ba..81cca9d 100644
--- a/src/common.sh

@@ -102,7 +102,7 @@ common::curl() {
     { code=$(curl -o "/proc/self/fd/${stdout}" -w '%{http_code}' "$@") || rv=$?; } {stdout}>&1

author: Jonathan Calmels
message: Add ENROOT_TRANSFER_RETRIES to retry network operations
patch:
commit 579873796d13958eb6ac67289183ce1476dca22f
Author: Jonathan Calmels <jcalmels@nvidia.com>
Date:   Wed Jun 10 20:25:03 2020 -0700

    Add ENROOT_TRANSFER_RETRIES to retry network operations
---
 conf/enroot.conf.in | 3 +++
 doc/cmd/import.md   | 1 +
 enroot.in           | 1 +
 src/docker.sh       | 4 ++--
 4 files changed, 7 insertions(+), 2 deletions(-)

diff --git a/conf/enroot.conf.in b/conf/enroot.conf.in
index baba514..eb67d2b 100644
--- a/conf/enroot.conf.in
+++ b/conf/enroot.conf.in
@@ -33,6 +33,9 @@
 # Maximum time in seconds to wait for network operations to complete (0 means unlimited).
 #ENROOT_TRANSFER_TIMEOUT    0
 
+# Number of times network operations should be retried.
+#ENROOT_TRANSFER_RETRIES    0
+
 # Use a login shell to run the container initialization.
 #ENROOT_LOGIN_SHELL         yes
 
diff --git a/doc/cmd/import.md b/doc/cmd/import.md
index 54324a2..b23097b 100644
--- a/doc/cmd/import.md
+++ b/doc/cmd/import.md
@@ -56,6 +56,7 @@ Requires the Docker CLI to communicate with the Docker daemon.
 | `ENROOT_MAX_CONNECTIONS` | `10` | Maximum number of concurrent connections (0 means unlimited) |
 | `ENROOT_CONNECT_TIMEOUT` | `30` | Maximum time in seconds to wait for connections establishment (0 means unlimited) |
 | `ENROOT_TRANSFER_TIMEOUT` | `0` | Maximum time in seconds to wait for network operations to complete (0 means unlimited) |
+| `ENROOT_TRANSFER_RETRIES` | `0` | Number of times network operations should be retried |
 | `ENROOT_ALLOW_HTTP` | `no` | Use HTTP for outgoing requests instead of HTTPS **(UNSECURE!)** |
 
 # Example
diff --git a/enroot.in b/enroot.in
index 608f74e..eda5a85 100644
--- a/enroot.in
+++ b/enroot.in
@@ -77,6 +77,7 @@ config::export ENROOT_MAX_PROCESSORS   "$(nproc)"
 config::export ENROOT_MAX_CONNECTIONS  10
 config::export ENROOT_CONNECT_TIMEOUT  30
 config::export ENROOT_TRANSFER_TIMEOUT 0
+config::export ENROOT_TRANSFER_RETRIES 0
 config::export ENROOT_LOGIN_SHELL      true
 config::export ENROOT_ALLOW_SUPERUSER  false
 config::export ENROOT_ALLOW_HTTP       false
diff --git a/src/docker.sh b/src/docker.sh
index 493c9f0..86e1229 100644
--- a/src/docker.sh
+++ b/src/docker.sh
@@ -19,10 +19,10 @@ readonly creds_file="${ENROOT_CONFIG_PATH}/.credentials"
 
 if [ -n "${ENROOT_ALLOW_HTTP-}" ]; then
     readonly curl_proto="http"
-    readonly curl_opts=("--proto" "=http,https" "--connect-timeout" "${ENROOT_CONNECT_TIMEOUT}" "--max-time" "${ENROOT_TRANSFER_TIMEOUT}" "-SsL")
+    readonly curl_opts=("--proto" "=http,https" "--retry" "${ENROOT_TRANSFER_RETRIES}" "--connect-timeout" "${ENROOT_CONNECT_TIMEOUT}" "--max-time" "${ENROOT_TRANSFER_TIMEOUT}" "-SsL")
 else
     readonly curl_proto="https"
-    readonly curl_opts=("--proto" "=https" "--connect-timeout" "${ENROOT_CONNECT_TIMEOUT}" "--max-time" "${ENROOT_TRANSFER_TIMEOUT}" "-SsL")
+    readonly curl_opts=("--proto" "=https" "--retry" "${ENROOT_TRANSFER_RETRIES}" "--connect-timeout" "${ENROOT_CONNECT_TIMEOUT}" "--max-time" "${ENROOT_TRANSFER_TIMEOUT}" "-SsL")
 fi
 
 docker::_authenticate() {

author: Jonathan Calmels
message: Use stime instead of start in ps output
patch:
commit daf540d8f26ae78034e4cd0f512f01d3f4e5dcdc
Author: Jonathan Calmels <jcalmels@nvidia.com>
Date:   Fri Jun 5 17:56:31 2020 -0700

    Use stime instead of start in ps output
---
 src/runtime.sh | 2 +-
 1 file changed, 1 insertion(+), 1 deletion(-)

diff --git a/src/runtime.sh b/src/runtime.sh
index 359a952..1f75cdb 100644
--- a/src/runtime.sh
+++ b/src/runtime.sh
@@ -522,7 +522,7 @@ runtime::list() {
             if [ "${#entry[@]}" -eq 0 ]; then
                 printf "%s\n" "${name}"
             else
-                ps -p "${entry[*]}" --no-headers -o pid:1,stat:1,start:1,etime:1,mntns:1,userns:1,command:1 \
+                ps -p "${entry[*]}" --no-headers -o pid:1,stat:1,stime:1,etime:1,mntns:1,userns:1,command:1 \
                   | awk -v name="${name}" '{
                       printf (NR==1) ? "%s\t" : " \t", name
                       printf "%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\t", $1, $2, $3, $4, $5, $6

author: Jonathan Calmels
message: Filter HCAs in sysfs and don't mount MOFED by default unless configured to do so
patch:
commit b0b80c436ae8f00bca00c13161e9ca07c514331a
Author: Jonathan Calmels <jcalmels@nvidia.com>
Date:   Mon May 18 23:51:15 2020 -0700

    Filter HCAs in sysfs and don't mount MOFED by default unless configured to do so
---
 conf/hooks/99-mellanox.sh | 19 +++++++++++++++++++
 enroot.in                 |  1 +
 2 files changed, 20 insertions(+)

diff --git a/conf/hooks/99-mellanox.sh b/conf/hooks/99-mellanox.sh
index f37144d..bfd4d31 100755
--- a/conf/hooks/99-mellanox.sh
+++ b/conf/hooks/99-mellanox.sh
@@ -22,6 +22,7 @@ fi
 
 declare -a drivers=()
 declare -a devices=()
+declare -a ifaces=()
 declare -A providers=()
 
 # Lookup all the devices and their respective driver.
@@ -34,6 +35,18 @@ for uevent in /sys/bus/pci/drivers/mlx?_core/*/infiniband_verbs/*/uevent; do
     devices+=("$(. "${uevent}"; echo "/dev/${DEVNAME}")")
 done
 
+# Lookup all the interfaces.
+for uevent in /sys/bus/pci/drivers/mlx?_core/*/infiniband/*/uevent; do
+    ifaces+=("$(. "${uevent}"; echo "${NAME}")")
+done
+
+# Hide all the device entries in sysfs by default.
+cat << EOF | enroot-mount --root "${ENROOT_ROOTFS}" -
+tmpfs /sys/class/infiniband tmpfs nosuid,noexec,nodev,mode=755,private
+tmpfs /sys/class/infiniband_verbs tmpfs nosuid,noexec,nodev,mode=755,private
+/sys/class/infiniband_verbs/abi_version /sys/class/infiniband_verbs/abi_version none x-create=file,bind,ro,nosuid,noexec,nodev,private
+EOF
+
 # Mount all the visible devices specified.
 if [ "${MELLANOX_VISIBLE_DEVICES}" = "all" ]; then
     MELLANOX_VISIBLE_DEVICES="$(seq -s, 0 $((${#devices[@]} - 1)))"
@@ -44,8 +57,14 @@ for id in ${MELLANOX_VISIBLE_DEVICES//,/ }; do
     fi
     providers["${drivers[id]}"]=true
     enroot-mount --root "${ENROOT_ROOTFS}" - <<< "${devices[id]} ${devices[id]} none x-create=file,bind,ro,nosuid,noexec,private"
+    ln -s "$(common::realpath "/sys/class/infiniband/${ifaces[id]}")" "${ENROOT_ROOTFS}/sys/class/infiniband/${ifaces[id]}"
+    ln -s "$(common::realpath "/sys/class/infiniband_verbs/${devices[id]##*/}")" "${ENROOT_ROOTFS}/sys/class/infiniband_verbs/${devices[id]##*/}"
 done
 
+if [ -z "${MELLANOX_MOUNT_DRIVER-}" ]; then
+    exit 0
+fi
+
 # Debian and its derivatives use a multiarch directory scheme.
 if [ -f "${ENROOT_ROOTFS}/etc/debian_version" ]; then
     : ${MELLANOX_PROVIDER_DIR:=/usr/lib/$(uname -m)-linux-gnu/libibverbs}
diff --git a/enroot.in b/enroot.in
index c8dd8fb..608f74e 100644
--- a/enroot.in
+++ b/enroot.in
@@ -38,6 +38,7 @@ config::init() {
     if [ -e "${file}" ]; then
         while IFS=$' \t=' read -r key value; do
             if [[ "${key}" =~ ^ENROOT_[[:upper:]_]+$ ]] ||
+               [[ "${key}" =~ ^(MELLANOX_|NVIDIA_) ]] ||
                [[ "${key}" =~ ^SSL_CERT_(DIR|FILE)$ ]] ||
                [[ "${key}" =~ ^(all|no|http|https)_proxy$ ]]; then
                 config::export "${key}" "$(eval echo "${value}")" "${overwrite}"

 # Debian and its derivatives use a multiarch directory scheme.
 if [ -f "${ENROOT_ROOTFS}/etc/debian_version" ]; then
+    : ${MELLANOX_PROVIDER_DIR:=/usr/lib/$(uname -m)-linux-gnu/libibverbs}
     readonly libdir="/usr/local/lib/$(uname -m)-linux-gnu/mellanox"
 else
+    : ${MELLANOX_PROVIDER_DIR:=/usr/lib64/libibverbs}
     readonly libdir="/usr/local/lib64/mellanox"
 fi
 mkdir -p "${ENROOT_ROOTFS}/${libdir}"
 
 for provider in "${!providers[@]}"; do
-    # Find each driver by reading its provider file.
-    if [ ! -f "${MELLANOX_IBVERBS_DIR}/${provider}.driver" ]; then
-        common::err "Provider driver not found: ${provider}"
+    # Find each driver by reading its provider config.
+    if [ ! -f "${MELLANOX_CONFIG_DIR}/${provider}.driver" ]; then
+        common::err "Provider config not found: ${provider}"
     fi
-    read -r x driver < "${MELLANOX_IBVERBS_DIR}/${provider}.driver"
+    read -r x driver < "${MELLANOX_CONFIG_DIR}/${provider}.driver"
     if [ -z "${driver}" ]; then
-        common::err "Could not parse provider file: ${MELLANOX_IBVERBS_DIR}/${provider}.driver"
+        common::err "Could not parse provider config: ${MELLANOX_CONFIG_DIR}/${provider}.driver"
+    fi
+    if [[ "${driver}" != /* ]]; then
+        driver="${MELLANOX_PROVIDER_DIR}/lib${driver}"
     fi
 
-    # Mount a copy of the provider file with a different driver path.
+    # Mount a copy of the provider config with a different driver path.
     printf "driver %s\n" "${libdir}/${driver##*/}" > "${ENROOT_RUNTIME_PATH}/${provider}.driver"
-    printf "%s %s none x-create=file,bind,ro,nosuid,nodev,noexec,private\n" "${ENROOT_RUNTIME_PATH}/${provider}.driver" "${MELLANOX_IBVERBS_DIR}/${provider}.driver"
+    printf "%s %s none x-create=file,bind,ro,nosuid,nodev,noexec,private\n" "${ENROOT_RUNTIME_PATH}/${provider}.driver" "${MELLANOX_CONFIG_DIR}/${provider}.driver"
 
     # Mount the latest driver (PABI).
     driver="$(set -- "" "${driver}"-*.so; echo "${@: -1}")"
+    if [ -z "${driver}" ]; then
+        common::err "Provider driver not found: ${provider}"
+    fi
     printf "%s %s none x-create=file,bind,ro,nosuid,nodev,private\n" "${driver}" "${libdir}/${driver##*/}"
 
     # Mount all the driver dependencies (except glibc).
